# NDA Playbook Matrix

Reference table from the Trinetix Contract Playbook. Use these as the deterministic clause-by-clause ceiling alongside the per-type SKILL.md.

| ID / Topic | Preferred position | Acceptable fallback | Red flags | Escalation | AI instruction |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NDA-001 Correct form | Use one-way NDA where Trinetix is the main disclosing party. Use MNDA where both parties exchange confidential information. | Client paper acceptable if no material red flags. | Wrong party roles; Trinetix only receiving despite disclosure; missing affiliates/personnel. | Legal review. | Identify whether disclosure is one-way or mutual. |
| NDA-002 Business Purpose | Purpose should be broad enough for discussions, business dealings, services, collaboration, and information exchange. | Narrow purpose acceptable if tied to a specific deal. | Purpose so narrow it blocks due diligence, affiliates, advisors, or delivery review. | Legal review. | Check purpose against planned use. |
| NDA-003 Definition of CI | Broad non-public information plus third-party information and derivatives; standard exclusions. | Accept marked/oral-confirmation limits only if business can operationalize. | Only marked information protected; no third-party/client info; no derivative materials; no trade secret protection. | Legal review. | Flag gaps in definition and exclusions. |
| NDA-004 Confidentiality period | Template uses 2 years after expiration/termination; trade secrets indefinite. | Accept 3-5 years. | Less than 2 years for sensitive commercial/technical information; no indefinite trade-secret protection. | Legal review. | Extract survival period and trade-secret rule. |
| NDA-005 Permitted recipients | Affiliates and personnel, including consultants and individual contractors, may receive information on need-to-know basis if bound by protective obligations. | Accept advisors/banks/tax authorities/professional consultants. | No affiliates/contractors; prior consent for every consultant; no professional advisors. | Escalate if operationally blocks deal evaluation/delivery. | Check recipient group and flow-down obligation. |
| NDA-006 Liability for personnel | Receiving party liable for unauthorized disclosure/misuse by affiliates, personnel, and third parties. | Accept commercially reasonable responsibility for representatives. | No responsibility for representatives; liability capped at nominal amount. | Legal review. | Flag representative responsibility limitations. |
| NDA-007 Deliverable examples / education | Template allows limited examples of deliverables if no proprietary information or for internal education/training. | Accept prior approval for external examples. | Total ban on internal training where needed; broad client right to use Trinetix examples without protection. | Sales/Legal review. | Flag portfolio/training restrictions. |
| NDA-008 Return/destruction | Return designated information within 10 business days; legal/compliance copy retained for dispute/legal compliance; licensed-use exception. | Accept reasonable destruction/certification process. | No legal retention copy; immediate purge of backups; impossible certification. | Legal/IT review. | Check retention exceptions and backup feasibility. |
| NDA-009 Subsequent agreement precedence | Later binding agreement with confidentiality terms prevails to conflict extent. | Accept if conflict scope is clear. | NDA overrides MSA in all cases and creates inconsistent obligations. | Legal review. | Check precedence against MSA. |
| NDA-010 Governing law / disputes | Tennessee law. One-way NDA gives Disclosing Party discretion between arbitration and litigation; MNDA uses AAA arbitration in Nashville after negotiations. | Accept neutral AAA/JAMS arbitration or agreed court with CLCO review. | Client home court only; unilateral client forum; foreign law without review. | CLCO approval. | Extract law, forum, arbitration, injunctive relief. |
| NDA-011 No hidden restrictive covenants | NDA should not contain non-compete, broad non-solicit, exclusivity, IP assignment, audit, security, or data-processing obligations unless reviewed. | Narrow no-circumvention only if business approves. | Employment non-solicit, client non-compete, ownership of ideas, broad residuals waiver. | CLCO review. | Search for hidden restrictive terms outside confidentiality section. |
